message:
feat(mvcc): HTM fast-path contract surface + unified EBR reclamation helpers

Two related additions to the MVCC machinery:

(1) New htm_fast_path module codifies the rollout shape for a
    hardware-transactional-memory (HTM) fast path across the flat
    combiner (flat_combining.rs) and the commit-sequence combiner
    (commit_combiner.rs) without actually switching either site
    over yet.  This is the pre-wiring contract from the
    HTM_GUARD_DESIGN.md design note.

(2) New reclamation module pulls the "advance epoch then drain
    the retire queue" sequence out of VersionStore's three open-
    coded call sites (advance_epoch, gc_tick, the priority-vacuum
    path) into a small set of helpers that return a structured
    EbrReclamationPass { observed_epoch, min_pinned_epoch,
    recycled_slots }.  This fixes a double-count in the EBR
    telemetry metrics and guarantees arena-slot recycling and
    epoch advancement happen under a consistent arena-write-lock
    window.

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/htm_fast_path.rs (new, ~354 lines)

- CombinerSite enum { FlatCombiner, CommitSequenceCombiner } with
  a label() helper, so telemetry and tests can distinguish which
  site a probe or retry was for.
- Contract surface types (names only, non-operative): probe
  result variants, retry policy knobs, integration hook trait.
- Module-level docs explicitly spell out the non-goals for this
  slice: no CPUID / platform intrinsics, no unsafe, no behavior
  change in flat_combining.rs or commit_combiner.rs, no new Cargo
  features.  This is deliberately the "shape" that later beads
  will fill in.

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/reclamation.rs (new, ~158 lines)

- EbrReclamationPass { observed_epoch: u64, min_pinned_epoch:
  Option<u64>, recycled_slots: usize } return type for any
  maintenance pass.
- drain_reclaimable_slots(registry, retire_queue, arena,
  target_epoch) — the core pump that asks the retire queue for
  the safe reclamation epoch given the guard registry's min
  pinned epoch, then recycles those arena slots in one shot.
- advance_epoch_and_reclaim(registry, retire_queue, arena) —
  advance the global epoch, observe the new current epoch, then
  call drain_reclaimable_slots at that epoch.
- reclaim_at_epoch(registry, retire_queue, arena, target_epoch)
  — drain only, for callers that already know the epoch to aim
  at (the priority-vacuum path).
- retire_and_reclaim(registry, retire_queue, arena,
  pruned_indices, retire_epoch) — retire a batch of pruned slot
  indices at the supplied retire_epoch and immediately attempt
  a reclamation pass, returning the combined result.

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lib.rs
- pub mod htm_fast_path; and pub mod reclamation; inserted in
  alphabetical order.
- Allow clippy::items_after_test_module on
  metadata_publication_contract_tests now that the test module
  is no longer the last item in the file.

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/ebr.rs
- EbrMetrics::retirements_deferred_total doc clarified: it
  counts *actual* Crossbeam-deferred retirements via defer_retire
  / defer_retire_with, not every call to EbrRetireQueue::retire.
  Previously the retire_batch() path was double-counting by also
  calling record_retirement_deferred() after the queue push.
- EbrRetireQueue::retire_batch() drops the trailing
  `for _ in 0..count { GLOBAL_EBR_METRICS.record_retirement_deferred() }`
  loop; the Crossbeam path is the only caller that should be
  recording into retirements_deferred_total.
- (Continued in the retire queue simplification: the safe-epoch
  computation is moved into drain_reclaimable_slots where the
  arena write lock is already held, so the open-coded dance here
  goes away.)

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/invariants.rs (VersionStore)
- advance_epoch(): body becomes a three-line wrapper that
  acquires arena.write() and delegates to
  advance_epoch_and_reclaim(guard_registry, retire_queue,
  arena), returning pass.recycled_slots.  Removes the open-coded
  `self.guard_registry.advance_epoch(); self.try_recycle_retired_slots(...)`
  sequence.
- gc_tick():
    - Split the "pre-pass" epoch advance into a dedicated
      advance_epoch_and_reclaim call under arena.write(), capture
      the observed epoch as retire_epoch, and drop the guard
      before entering gc_tick_with_registry so the prune phase
      can reacquire it.
    - After the prune phase, if result.pruned_indices is
      non-empty, open a second arena.write() guard and delegate
      to retire_and_reclaim(..., result.pruned_indices.iter().copied(),
      retire_epoch).  The tracing::debug! payload now records
      retire_epoch, recycle_epoch (post-pass observed_epoch),
      recycled_slots, and post_pass.min_pinned_epoch in a single
      structured event instead of two loose lines.
- priority_vacuum-style tick (same file, further down): switch
  the "advance to a specific target epoch and drain" path over
  to reclaim_at_epoch(..., current_epoch).  Behavior is
  identical; this deletes the open-coded call to
  self.guard_registry.advance_epoch_to and the subsequent
  try_recycle_retired_slots(observed_epoch).

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lifecycle.rs, writer_routing_telemetry.rs
- Minor follow-ons: adopt the new reclamation helpers in the
  transaction abort / writer routing paths so every place that
  drops an arena slot goes through the same structured maintenance
  pass.  No behavior change for the writer routing telemetry
  counters themselves; those still increment on routing decisions.

Together the reclamation helper + the ebr doc/metric fix should
eliminate the long-standing "retirements_deferred_total is roughly
2x retirements_pushed_total" anomaly in GC dashboards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
